source.page_label: Hunt By Species: Migratory Birds | Montana FWP
source.update_note: 2026-27 migratory bird data from Montana FWP migratory bird page. Montana has three waterfowl flyways: Central Flyway Zone 1, Central Flyway Zone 2 (split season), and Pacific Flyway.

species.name: Duck (including Mergansers)
species.season_raw: See zones below for split season dates
species.hunting_units: Central Flyway Zones 1, 2, and Pacific Flyway

species.name: Goose
species.season_raw: See zones below for split season dates
species.hunting_units: Central Flyway Zones 1, 2, and Pacific Flyway

species.name: Coot
species.season_raw: Same dates as ducks by zone
species.hunting_units: Central Flyway Zones 1, 2, and Pacific Flyway

species.name: Sandhill Crane
species.season_raw: OTC mid-continent: Sept 28 – Oct 31; Special Permit (Rocky Mtn): Sept 1 – Oct 31

species.name: Swan (Tundra/Trumpeter)
species.season_raw: Central Flyway: Oct 3 – Jan 8; Pacific Flyway: Oct 10 – Dec 5
species.hunting_units: Central Flyway and Pacific Flyway (drawing only)

species.name: Youth Waterfowl
species.bag_limit: Same as regular season limits for ducks, geese, coots

licenses.name: Montana Conservation License
licenses.covers: Required for all hunters (ages 12+); supports conservation programs

licenses.name: Base Hunting License
licenses.covers: Required for all hunters along with Conservation License

licenses.name: Montana Migratory Bird License
licenses.covers: Required for all migratory bird hunting (doves, ducks, geese, swans, cranes, snipe, coots)

licenses.name: Federal Duck Stamp (Migratory Bird Hunting Stamp)
licenses.covers: Required for waterfowl hunters age 16+ (ducks, geese, mergansers, swans, coots)

licenses.name: Sandhill Crane License (Drawing)
licenses.covers: Permit to hunt sandhill cranes (drawing, May 1 – June 1)

licenses.name: Swan Hunting License (Drawing)
licenses.covers: Permit to hunt swans (drawing, May 1 – June 1)

licenses.name: Youth Migratory Bird License (Ages 12-17)
licenses.covers: Same as MT Migratory Bird License at reduced cost for youth

key_notes.title: All migratory bird hunters in Montana must complete HIP (Harvest Information Program) certification before purchasing a Montana Migratory Bird License.

sources.label: Montana FWP — Migratory Bird Regulations
sources.evidence: HIP Requirement: All migratory bird hunters in Montana must complete a survey (name, address, date of birth) before purchasing the Montana Migratory Bird License.

key_notes.title: A Federal Duck Stamp ($25) is required for all waterfowl hunters age 16 or older (ducks, geese, mergansers, swans, coots). Not required during Spring Light Goose Conservation Order.
applies_to: Federal Duck Stamp (Migratory Bird Hunting Stamp)

sources.label: Montana FWP — Migratory Bird Regulations
sources.evidence: Federal Migratory Bird Stamp required for individuals 16 and older (both resident and nonresident). Available from USPS and all FWP offices.

key_notes.title: Central Flyway is divided into two zones. Zone 1 includes all Central Flyway counties except Zone 2. Zone 2 includes: Big Horn, Carbon, Custer, Prairie, Rosebud, Stillwater, Sweet Grass, Treasure, Yellowstone counties.

sources.label: Montana FWP 2026 Migratory Bird Regulations (PDF)
sources.evidence: Central Flyway divided into two zones (map on page 8) with different season dates. Zone 1: All other Central Flyway counties. Zone 2: Big Horn, Carbon, Custer, Prairie, Rosebud, Stillwater, Sweet Grass, Treasure, Yellowstone.

key_notes.title: Sandhill crane and swan licenses are available through a drawing application process (May 1 – June 1). Must possess valid Conservation, Base Hunting, and MT Migratory Bird License to apply.

sources.label: Montana FWP 2026 Migratory Bird Regulations (PDF)
sources.evidence: Drawing Applications online at ols.fwp.mt.gov by 11:45 p.m. MDT: Sandhill crane: May 1 – June 1; Swan: May 1 – June 1. Application fees: $10 (resident), $50 (nonresident). Must possess current Conservation, Base Hunting, and MT Migratory Bird License to apply.

key_notes.title: Duck bag limits have species-specific restrictions: 6 daily in aggregate. Check FWP PDF for specific species limits (mallards, pintails, canvasbacks, etc.).

sources.label: Montana FWP 2026 Migratory Bird Regulations (PDF)
sources.evidence: The daily bag limit is 6 ducks or mergansers. The daily bag limit for coots is 15.

key_notes.title: 72-hour swan reporting: Federal law requires hunters who shoot a swan in either flyway to complete and return a Swan Bill Measurement Card within 72 hours of harvest.

sources.label: Montana FWP 2026 Migratory Bird Regulations (PDF)
sources.evidence: 72-hour swan reporting: Federal law requires hunters who shoot a swan in either flyway to complete and return a Swan Bill Measurement Card within 72 hours of harvest.

key_notes.title: Authorized shooting hours: one-half hour before sunrise to sunset each day. Use official sunrise-sunset tables.

sources.label: Montana FWP 2026 Migratory Bird Regulations (PDF)
sources.evidence: Authorized shooting hours: one-half hour before sunrise to sunset each day. Use official sunrise-sunset tables.
